assumptions: The question concerns standard consolidated financial statements of a non-investment entity.
The intragroup dividend remains an unsettled receivable/payable so it can be assessed as an intragroup monetary item.

approaches.label: Hedge of qualifying intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale: IFRS 9 permits foreign currency risk on an intragroup monetary item in consolidated financial statements if the resulting exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
conditions: The dividend receivable/payable is an intragroup monetary item.
The entities have different functional currencies such that the related foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
The hedge is formally designated and documented and meets the IFRS 9 hedge effectiveness requirements.

approaches.label: General exclusion of intragroup items in consolidation
approaches.rationale: IFRS 9 generally allows only exposures with parties external to the reporting entity to be designated as hedged items in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Hedge of qualifying forecast intragroup transaction
approaches.rationale: IFRS 9 has a separate exception for highly probable forecast intragroup transactions affecting consolidated profit or loss, but that does not match an already recognised dividend receivable.
conditions: The transaction must be highly probable rather than already recognised.
It must be denominated in a currency other than the functional currency of the entity entering into the transaction.
The foreign currency risk must affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale: IFRIC 16 applies only to hedges of net investments in foreign operations and states that it should not be applied by analogy to other hedge accounting relationships.
conditions: The hedged item would need to be an amount of net assets of a foreign operation.
The relationship would need to satisfy the IFRS 9 designation, documentation and effectiveness requirements for a net investment hedge.
